title: $η$-pairing in a two-band model of spinless fermions
abstract: We study the two-band model of spinless fermions in which itinerant fermions interact with localized fermions through the two-particle hybridization. In 1D version, the model has exact solution using the Bethe ansatz. It has been shown that accounting for two-particle hybridization reduces the repulsive interaction between itinerant fermions. In the case of strong interaction, the effective interaction between itinerant fermions is attractive, and $η$-pairing of spinless fermions is realized. The proposed pairing mechanism via two-particle hybridization can lead to $p$-superconducting states with $η$-pairing. $η$-pairing of spinless fermions could explain the phenomenon of high-temperature superconductivity experimentally observed in hydrogen-rich materials at high pressures.
summary: This paper explores a mechanism for superconductivity in a two-band model of spinless fermions. By using two-particle hybridization between itinerant and localized bands, the model demonstrates that repulsive interactions can be transformed into an effective attractive interaction. This process leads to the formation of eta-pairing and p-type superconducting states. The findings are particularly relevant for understanding high-temperature superconductivity in hydrogen-rich materials under high pressure.
main_problem: Investigating whether two-particle hybridization between itinerant and localized fermions can induce an effective attractive interaction to realize eta-pairing and p-type superconductivity.
main_result: The study finds that two-particle hybridization reduces repulsive interactions between itinerant fermions, leading to an effective attractive interaction and the realization of eta-pairing and p-type superconducting states.
method: The 1D version of the model is solved exactly using the Bethe ansatz, involving the derivation of the two-particle scattering matrix and the analysis of the thermodynamic limit via rapidities.
model_or_system: A 1D two-band model of spinless fermions consisting of itinerant s-band fermions and localized d-band fermions, featuring two-particle s-d hybridization.
key_observables: eta-pairing operators, pair energy (epsilon_plus/minus), off-diagonal long-range order (ODLRO), and critical temperature (Tc).
important_parameters: hybridization strength (g), intra-band interactions (J, I), localized fermion energy (epsilon), and Fermi energy (epsilon_F).
main_assumptions: The effective interaction is treated as a constant near the Fermi energy; the model is restricted to a 1D version for the exact solution.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates the energy levels of the 2D system.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the two-band model and its Hamiltonian, applies the Bethe ansatz to find exact solutions, derives the conditions for eta-pairing and Bose condensation, and discusses the implications for p-type superconductivity and high-pressure hydrogen-rich materials.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
